profiles.bio: Gustavo is a senior executive with C-Level responsibilities since 2009. He joined A1 Engenharia (a family group) after graduating from Universidade Federal do Paraná in 2008 and was promoted to lead the Manufacturing firm after the severe effects of the World Financial Crisis. Following a successful turnaround and sustainable growth thereafter, he later co-directed a Steam Turbine factory. During his career, Gustavo has taken on several roles including leading investment analysis, M&As, lawsuits, union agreements, and corporate governance enhancements. This extensive track-record of senior management responsibilities converged in a deep experience of companies’ full business cycles from sales to cash flows through the entire operational chain. Consequently, Gustavo was invited to join the first Board of Directors of A1 Engenharia and became an advisor in the family’s other endeavours in Agribusiness and Energy Generation. Notwithstanding, he conducted businesses in a very volatile environment regarding supply/demand, inflation, regulations, and political stability. A strong believer that businesses should perform anchored on values of respect, trust, motivation, and dedication, he focused efforts on technical developments such as Brazil’s first steam turbines for waste heat energy recovery, CSP (concentrated solar power) and waste to energy. Outside his principal company roles,  Gustavo co-founded an NGO focused on sustainable farming and invested in a Health-tech start up aimed at mental health. He is a thoughtful, charismatic leader who constantly defies the status quo and pursues innovation as a means to transform businesses.
career_highlights: At the age of 24, directed a 9-month corporate turnaround subsequent to WFC and 4 years of compounded losses. Results were achieved through a major overhaul in the business strategy within

profiles.name: Nirina Rajaonary
profiles.industry: Manufacturing | FMCG | Paper, Industry | Hospitality, New Business Development, | Strategy | General, Management
profiles.education: Sloan 2023, London Business School, MBA in Hospitality, Management,, ESSEC Business School, BSc in Hospitality, Management,, Ecole Management Savignac
profiles.bio: After an extremely successful career spanning 14 years in the luxury hospitality industry, across France, Mauritius, the United Arab Emirates & Djibouti, working for premier companies like Relais & Châteaux hotels, One & Only Resorts, and Kempinski Hotels and Resorts, Nirina returned to his roots. Once in Madagascar, in 2010, he was surprised to learn that all the toilet paper in his country was being imported. Sensing an opportunity, set out on a journey of creating Société de Production d’Articles Hygiéniques (S.P.A.H), the only tissue paper mill in Madagascar and the Indian Ocean region to use recycled paper as raw materials. Despite no background in the industry, he built a successful business from a scratch.  He participated in all the important stages of the business which included sourcing a supplier for the production line from China, securing funding, importing required materials and machinery, installation and commissioning of the production line, staff recruitment and training, creating and growing the brand, and establishing a distribution network. Within 5 years, his brainchild became the industry leader within the country, with a market share of 45%, and an EBITDA margin of 34% . More importantly, they recycled over c.1,500 tons of waste paper per year and provided employment to 95 people. He successfully exited from the company in December 2022 to pursue the Sloan programme at London Business School. Nirina was also actively involved in the development of the private sector in Madagascar as President of Jeune Patronat de Madagascar and the Vice-President of the Groupement des Entreprises de Madagascar which is the largest employers’ organisation of Madagascar accounting for 45% of national added value and 77% of all taxes.
career_highlights: Founded a company that became a distinguished model of a sustainable social enterprise in Madagascar and the Indian Ocean region.
Pioneered the creation of a sustainable network for collection and recycling of waste paper and cardboards in the country.

Chaired the steering committee for creating the “White Paper” for the Malagasy private sector which formulated the long-term vision for economic development by the private sector based on the values of social responsibility and inclusiveness amongst others.

profiles.name: Samer Seguias
profiles.industry: Management Consulting |, Financial Services, Strategy & Planning | Business, & Data Transformation |, Business Architecture
profiles.education: Sloan 2023, London Business School, MBA, Boston University, BBA, American Uiversity of, Beirut
profiles.bio: Samer is a business transformation, data & digital transformation, and strategy leader in Financial Services. In his latest role he was responsible for Business Architecture and Data Product Management in the Wealth Management space. He established data and platforms strategy, ensuring alignment between business transformation and data, leading multiple product teams in building new and innovative data products and platforms, and improving processes to increase stability and performance. Prior to that he was a Management Consultant providing advisory services focused on strategy, digital transformation, and data platforms for Fortune 500 companies. His clients included HSBC, Ernst & Young, Moody’s, Options Clearing Corporation, and others. He has a successful track record of delivery and building successful partnerships with cross functional teams, client partners, and C-Level executives. He has led and managed large multicultural teams across geographies and regions and have business experience in the Middle East, North America, Europe, North Africa, and Asia. After Sloan, Samer is looking to continue transforming financial services organisations and helping them leverage new and innovative strategies and solutions to achieve growth.
career_highlights: Responsible for Business Architecture, Data Product Management, and Financial Data Platforms for a Wealth Management firm with over $150 Billion in AUM.

profiles.name: Shouvik Sen
profiles.industry: 24 Years in Indian Army across, multiple functions leading, teams from 6 to 600, Supply Chain | Procurement, | Budgeting | Op Logistics |, Stakeholder Engagement |, Crisis Management
profiles.education: Sloan 2023, London Business School, Masters in Management,, Osmania University, Masters in Weapons Tech,, Defence Inst of Advanced Tech
profiles.bio: Shouvik is an articulate leader who brings a wealth of transferable skills from a long military career into the business world. Having led very large teams to deliver critical projects under ambiguous conditions, his cross functional experience transcending domains can be valuable across industries that seek resources to solve problems while delivering assured, high impact results.  He was selected for a military diplomatic engagement to represent India in Tajikistan as the Team leader on the basis of demonstated track record. Shouvik has been engaging with stakeholders across the spectrum of nationality, seniority, intelligence and capability towards alignment with organisational goals and successful mission implementation. He has dealt with procurement of operational inventory, last mile connectivity in supply chains, planned and executed disaster relief and mitigation while handling operations with substantial budgets. Lucid communication skills, analytical and logical thinking during a crisis and adaptability built up over 24 years handling a diverse portfolio of situations makes him an asset on any team. A blend of military leadership, decision making and business skills polished on the Sloan Programme lends him an unique perspective on challenges and problems of the business world. Shouvik is keen to use his skills in the business world to add value to teams that seek to create impact either in consulting, general management or roles related to sustainability and governance.
career_highlights: Led a 600 member cross functional team as Commanding Officer. Laid down strategic vision in conflict zone, delivering secure lines of communication with zero loss of life. All HR and operations functions

profiles.name: Gauri Singh
profiles.industry: Banking & Financial Services |, Start Up, Strategy Formulation &, Execution | Sales & Operations  |, Channel Management
profiles.education: Sloan 2023, London Business School, BA(Hons) Economics, MBA (Finance)
profiles.bio: A  results-driven business leader, Gauri has over 19 years of successful experience in consumer banking across diverse product lines, geographies and business functions. With deep industry expertise, she has a proven track record in leading large front end sales teams and running strategic business roles . She believes in purposeful leadership with a commitment to create and drive lasting change. Starting her career journey with HDFC BANK as a management trainee, she grew into a versatile business leader managing one of the largest regions in the country with a retail liability book of $4.4bn . Prior to joining the London Business School Sloan Fellowship programme, Gauri was heading the Employee Banking & Financial Solutions Business for North Region, a new business channel that became one of highest revenue drivers and a strategic area of growth for the bank in India. Gauri has held various leadership roles in retail banking, wealth , product and channel management .She has led development of strategic roadmaps, grew market share, expanded branch network, managed large-scale operations oversaw fraud and compliance. Her entrepreneurial mindset is deeply rooted in her perpetual need for learning and nurturing innovation. With a proven ability to solve complex business problems she enjoys working in challenging situations and firmly believes in treating equity as a fundamental component of strong leadership and cultivating a culture to include and empower teams.

profiles.name: Deddy Sudarijanto
profiles.position: Senior Executive | Impact Enterpreneur | Indika Group
profiles.industry: Strategic Consulting |, Private Equity | Energy |, Broadcasting | Chemical |, Airlines | Retailing | Hospitality, Strategy | Investment |, Turnaround Management |, Learning Organisation
profiles.education: Sloan 2023, London Business School, MS Industrial Engineering,, Stanford University, BS Industrial Engineering,, Northeastern University
profiles.bio: Deddy is a seasoned senior executive with extensive experience in strategic consulting, private equity investment and turnaround management.  He was a founding member and valued culture carrier for McKinsey Indonesia.  In addition to advising businesses on strategic issues, his work with McKinsey Global Institute and the World Bank, advising the Government of Indonesia, resulted in Indonesia’s electronics industry strategic development blueprint. As the Indonesia Country Head for Standard Chartered Private Equity Limited, he managed its USD300 million investment portfolio and served as Non-Executive Director in multiple investee companies. As a senior executive in Indika, a leading Indonesian-based family office, Deddy served as CEO for multiple business units in chemical, entertainment and television broadcasting industries executing M&A, LBOs, turnaround management, capital raising and public listing. Deddy co-founded impact-oriented SMBs in hospitality, sustainable housing, telecom retailing and nationwide BPO services employing 4000+ salesforce primarily from underprivileged backgrounds. Married for 25 years with three children, Deddy is a family man who loves travelling, history, arts, theatre, and music.  An avid explorer, he trekked to Mount Everest Base Camp with his wife in 2015. Post-LBS, Deddy is excited about the opportunities to collaborate with other fellow leaders to build value-driven enterprises grounded on a strong learning and innovation culture to deliver impact to society.
career_highlights: McKinsey & Co Indonesia founding member advising government, MNCs and national clients from telecommunications, pulp and paper, automotive, cement, and education industries in Indonesia, Malaysia and Thailand on strategy, restructuring and turnaround.

profiles.name: Yasuo Suzuki
profiles.industry: Commercial Bank, Strategy | Strategic Partner, Relationship Management |, Corporate Banking
profiles.education: Sloan 2023, London Business School, Bachelor of Arts (Language and, Area Studies), Tokyo University, of Foreign Studies
profiles.bio: Yasuo Suzuki is a senior banker with 17 years of professional experience in corporate banking and strategic planning. He works for MUFG Bank, the commercial banking arm of MUFG (Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group), the leading financial conglomerate in Japan. As a seasoned corporate banker, Yasuo has built a strong relationship with his customers. He has supported their business expansion and turnaround plans by providing sound and viable financial solutions. His keen intellect and analytical mindset helped to identify customers’ hidden needs, evaluate their credibility, and take risks accordingly. During his almost five-year experience in the strategic planning division, Yasuo was instrumental in enhancing the corporate value of two affiliate banks in Vietnam and the Philippines. By demonstrating and justifying the meaningfulness of these investments to top management, he played a pivotal role in generating synergies with these investees. He leveraged his strong interpersonal skills by successfully engaging various stakeholders with conflicting interests. Yasuo is extremely passionate and his resilience, combined with mental agility helps him in achieving meaningful goals even in a challenging environment. His excellent organizational skills and ability to think logically, identifying the root cause by testing his assumptions enables him to align the interests of the relevant stakeholders. He also has extensive international experience in Singapore and China. Yasuo always ensures communications are honest and open and walks the talk as a senior team member, leading a team with different cultural backgrounds.

profiles.name: Yerlan Tuleugozhin
profiles.position: Strategy & Finance & Operations | Oil & Gas | Metals & Mining
profiles.industry: Metals & Mining | Oil & Gas, Upstream | Chemicals |, Consulting, Corporate strategy | Portfolio, management | M&A | ESG |, Corporate finance | Valuation I, Corporate governance | Project, management
profiles.education: Sloan 2023, London Business School, Master’s in Finance,, London Business School, B.Sc, Economics and, Management
profiles.bio: Yerlan Tuleugozhin is a C-suite professional with over 18 years of management and leadership experience in extractive industries, mainly in Metal & Mining and Oil & Gas (upstream) sectors. Yerlan has previously held diverse leadership roles (CFO, CSO and others) in areas of financial management, corporate finance, corporate strategy and asset management, and most recently served as Chief Strategy Officer at global uranium mining leader, Kazatomprom, where he was particularly responsible for strategic development, asset management and driving corporate ESG agenda and efforts. Yerlan has led implementation of operations excellence and cost optimisation initiatives during period of low oil prices, successfully led and managed key Metal & Mining deals related to uranium mining assets and their subsoil contracts. He has also successfully implemented turnaround and divestment processes of number of various assets such as drilling entity, caustic chemical plant, silicon mining asset, tantalum-beryllium plant and other non-core assets for Metal & Mining industry.  Moreover he led corporate capital investment and sustainability committees and advocated important issues and major projects at the Board investment/audit committees. Yerlan began his career as auditor at Ernst & Young and then shifted to Oil & Gas industry, where he held a number of finance-related roles and senior management positions, including cost controller and CFO position in the largest Oil & Gas joint ventures in Kazakhstan. He then moved to a uranium mining Joint Venture with French giant Orano, where he served as a deputy CFO before moving smoothly to the role of Managing Director of JVs and later appointed as a Chief Strategy Officer at Kazatomprom, the world’s largest uranium producer.

profiles.name: Mark Webster
profiles.industry: Banking | Construction, compliance | Renewable, energy | Small business, Founder | Board membership |, Sales | Compliance
profiles.education: Sloan 2023, London Business School, Goldsmith College, University of, London
profiles.bio: Mark is an entrepreneurial and energetic business leader with over 25 years of international experience at top-tier global finance firms. He has invested in, founded, bought, and sold multiple businesses across a large spectrum of fields. He has acted as a hands-on leader and held non-executive positions, positively impacting businesses to achieve their goals. His meticulous focus on details and deep technical understanding is complemented by genuine creative input that enables him to build and grow innovative, profitable, customer- facing businesses in highly competitive markets. In his role as CEO of Bottlepay, Mark led the development and launch of a payment’s platform at the forefront of blockchain and digital technology that combines utility, flexibility, and simplicity. During this time, he successfully completed one of the largest seed funding rounds of the year, which was oversubscribed, and he oversaw the sale of the company. With many years of experience in global leadership roles for blue chip banks, Mark built and developed businesses driven by service excellence and bolstered by rigorous control frameworks. Their success has always been founded on the coherence of motivated teams, who share a common vision and ambition. He prides himself on his ability to engage and influence key stakeholders at all levels of an organization and invests time and effort into establishing a culture shaped by customer focus and collaboration. Mark is a strong communicator, able to convey value propositions accessibly to a variety of audiences and give clarity and impact to thought leadership in both a business’s priorities and strategy.

profiles.name: Enyuan Xue
profiles.industry: Private Equity | Consumer, Goods | Retail, CEO | CFO | Executive Director |, Business Start-up |, Strategy | Planning | Operations
profiles.education: Sloan 2023, London Business School, Masters in Finance, CFA
profiles.bio: Enyuan has a solid track record of running Private Equity portfolio companies and creating significant value. Working with PE funds, he has managed businesses at different stages - including startups, rapid expansions, and liquidations. He has demonstrated ability to handle challenging situations, including experience in building large-scale business within a short time frame, stabilizing businesses under pressure, managing shareholder conflicts and detecting financial fraud. Enyuan is always eager to explore new areas and has been a first- generation player in various fields in China including futures floor trading, strategic consulting, privatization of listed companies, and operational roles for PE portfolio companies. Enyuan is the Managing Director of Zebra, a new technology private equity firm that focuses on creating value through operational control and fundamental research. He has also held leadership positions in some of China’s leading companies including Shanghai Reliance Group, a leading environmental service and facility management company, and Yum China, China’s most successful consumer goods company operating brands such as KFC, Pizza Hut and Taco Bell.

profiles.name: Taka Yamaoka
profiles.industry: Investment Bank, Global HR |, Corporate Governance |, Capital management in UK
profiles.education: Sloan 2023, London Business School, Bachelor of Commerce and, Administration, Victoria, University of Wellington
profiles.bio: Taka (Takahisa) Yamaoka is a senior banker with 15 years of experience. He currently works for Mitsubishi UFJ Securities Holdings, wholly owned by the Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group, Japan’s largest financial conglomerate. Taka has gained cross-functional exposure by spending the majority of his career working in the corporate centre in Japan and the UK. Taka is passionate and a strong relationship builder with high self-awareness and emotional intelligence. In Global HR, he was de facto number two in the global rewards’ team, across different countries with a matrix reporting structure. In addition, he developed the consistency bonus scheme, for all entities and branches outside of Japan. In Global Finance, he implemented the strategic support of the financial strategy, optimizing capital and liquidity resources for the UK subsidiary. Leveraging his solid expertise in regulatory capital management, subsidiary management and financial accounting, from head office. At the UK subsidiary of his previous firm, Taka joined the executive committee and provided comprehensive guidance, from the parent company’s perspective. He also assisted in establishing a corporate governance framework, in a project team, to integrate the London Bank and Securities entities for Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group.
career_highlights: Achieved a new bonus scheme for 24,000 employees outside of Japan, aiming to transform the business model from the local entity- wide to the business group with 15 of our worldwide global reward members.

profiles.name: Raffaella Zavattaro
profiles.industry: Automotive | Food & Beverage |, Heat-exchange | Not-for-Profit, Corporate Governance |, Leadership & Strategy |, Controlling | Project, Management | Sustainability
profiles.education: Sloan 2023, London Business School, Master in Public Policy and, Social change, UNITO, MBA, Bocconi, Milano, BA International Relations
profiles.bio: Raffaella is an entrepreneur with twenty-five years of experience. She started in the family business as a controller and then moved on to roles of increasing responsibility, up to her current role which sees her engaged with total business responsibility. During her professional career, Raffaella enhanced her business skills by obtaining an MBA and a Masters degree in Public Policy and Social Change. She is passionate about socio-economic causes and dedicates some of her time to tackling social inequalities. Raffaella has great communication skills which she displays clearly, concisely and confidently. She is a great leader who enjoys solving complex problems, while facilitating collaboration and motivating her team to ensure everyone feels a sense of  ownership in the final decision. She delegates efficiently and is a strong believer in empowering others. She is adept at using qualitative and quantitative methods to interpret and evaluate socio-economic phenomena. Her greatest strength is in her strategic thinking; “the big picture mindset”. As a result of her problem solving skills, Raffaella is able to thoroughly analyse relevant issues, assess possible risks, communicate her views and come to a solution that delivers the best strategic choice for the company. Raffaella is an innovator – she believes in process improvement through advanced technology that delivers a superior customer service and sustainable impact for all stakeholders.
career_highlights: Launched several innovation projects in collaboration with prominent engineering universities, helped preparation of technical documentation, involved suppliers and partners, keeping the team focused on results, budget and deadline. Improved sales by 20%.

profiles.name: Emerson Zou
profiles.industry: Luxury Fashion | Hospitality, General management | Luxury, retailing | Business scaling |, Asian consumer market
profiles.education: Sloan 2023, London Business School, Bachelor of Business, Administration in Hospitality,, Les Roches, Switzerland
profiles.bio: Emerson is a business leader with nearly 20 years of experience in the luxury fashion and hospitality industry. He has held leadership positions at prestigious companies such as Christian Louboutin, Loewe (LVMH group), Saint Laurent (Kering group), Prada, and Ermenegildo Zegna, where he consistently demonstrated a proven track record in scaling and turning around businesses, building and leading multifunctional and multinational teams, and crafting agile strategies throughout various phases of brand development. As General Manager of Asia for Christian Louboutin, Emerson served as the board-facing P&L owner and was responsible for overseeing the company’s business across eight Asian countries. Prior to his role at Christian Louboutin, Emerson held key leadership positions at Loewe (LVMH group) and Saint Laurent (Kering group), where he was fully responsible for top-line sales revenue in the China market. Previously, as part of retail management team at Prada and Zegna, he rose from a store manager to an area manager overseeing 10 stores. After completing his degree at Swiss hotel school Les Roches and management traineeship with some leading hotel chains in 2003, Emerson began his career in hospitality with Sofitel Hotels, where he was recognized as the youngest talent to be promoted to department head within the ACCOR group in China, before he transitioned to the luxury fashion industry in 2007. Emerson seeks opportunities as Asia Pacific Zone President in leading luxury brands, or alternatively a consulting role in the luxury sector.
